text.title: AWS CLI Compute Core Rules
text.summary: 5+ testable rules grounding the methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale. Includes the skip-this-methodology rule that the decision-tree maps to.

rule.statement: Every script MUST set `--profile` and `--region` explicitly (or rely on `AWS_PROFILE` + `AWS_REGION` env vars that the script asserts are present). Default-profile + inherited-region operations are forbidden in shared scripts.

rationale.source: AWS CLI v2 docs; faion-net ops practice

rule.rationale: Default-profile scripts cause silent cross-account or cross-region operations during incidents. Explicit profile + region make blast radius visible to the operator at command-read time.

rule.statement: Scripts MUST emit JSON output (`--output json` or `--query` projections) for any value consumed downstream. Human-formatted (`text` / `table`) output is forbidden for piped consumption.
rule.rationale: Text/table output breaks downstream JSON parsers and varies across AWS CLI versions. JSON is stable and parser-friendly.

rule.statement: Scripts MUST use `aws ec2 wait` / `aws lambda wait` / `aws s3api wait` instead of `sleep N` between state transitions. Custom polling loops require a fail-after-N-attempts gate.
rule.rationale: `sleep` either over-pauses (slow scripts) or under-pauses (race condition). Waiters poll the actual API state and exit promptly when the condition is reached.

rule.statement: Scripts MUST NOT contain inline credentials (access key, secret key, session token). Credentials come from named profile, IAM role assumption via OIDC, or instance profile. `--secret-access-key` flag is FORBIDDEN.

rationale.source: AWS security best practices; GitHub OIDC for AWS

rule.rationale: Embedded credentials leak into version control and CI logs. OIDC + named profiles + role assumption are the modern AWS authentication baseline.

rule.statement: Apply AWS CLI scripts to one operation type per script (EC2 OR S3 OR Lambda). Multi-service operations MUST be split. Skip when scripted automation should run as IaC instead — use aws-cfn-terraform-templates.
rule.rationale: Anti-conflation: multi-service scripts hide failure modes per service and complicate retry semantics. CLI is for ephemeral / one-shot ops; IaC handles repeatable deployments.
